meta.title: Texas — VA facilities & veteran contacts (test page)
meta.disclaimer:
Phones, addresses, and hours are compiled from public VA.gov and state pages. Always confirm on the official site or by phone before you travel—especially for holidays and weather closures.

This page is general educational information only. It is not legal advice, not medical advice, and Clarity4Vets does not represent anyone before VA or any other agency. Distances are straight-line approximations, not driving time. Map and directions links use third-party services for convenience only—verify every detail with official sources. Listing a facility or organization does not imply endorsement or a recommendation to use a particular service for claims or benefits.

startingPoint.label: Default starting point (demo)
startingPoint.addressLine: ZIP 77355 — approximate postal area (not a street address)
startingPoint.privacyNote: Coordinates are a rounded ZIP-level center for demos. Use “Your Texas ZIP” above to center the map on your own area—only an approximate postal center is used, not a street address.

sources.label: Coverage: 75 Texas VA facilities from official VA.gov system location pages (North Texas, Central Texas, South Texas, Houston, West Texas, Texas Valley, El Paso; New Mexico sites excluded). Geocoded Apr 2026.

scheduling.label: VA North Texas — appointments
scheduling.note: From VA North Texas contact page (Jan 2026).

scheduling.label: VA Central Texas — main line / after hours
scheduling.note: System-wide; after hours ask for administrator on duty per VA.gov.

organizations.name: Texas Veterans Commission (state)
organizations.hoursNote: State benefits and claims counseling (not federal VA). Verify current hours before visiting a regional office.
organizations.sourceNote: tvc.texas.gov — toll-free and Austin HQ mail.

organizations.name: VFW Post 6796 (John Franklin Sprague / Dallas)
organizations.hoursNote: Many posts open afternoons/evenings; call ahead—hours vary by day and events.
organizations.sourceNote: Public post information; confirm before you drive.

organizations.name: DAV veteran service office (San Antonio area — sample listing)
organizations.hoursNote: Call before you go—hours and walk-in rules change. Not a federal VA office.
organizations.sourceNote: Public directory-style listing; confirm on davtexas.org or dav.org before relying on it.

emergencyCopy.title: If you are in crisis
lines: Dial 988, then press 1 for the Veterans Crisis Line.
If this is a life-threatening emergency, call 911.
emergencyCopy.disclaimer: If you are in immediate danger, call 911. Crisis numbers here are general public information—confirm current contact options on official VA and 988 sites. This list is not a substitute for emergency services or professional crisis care.
